reasoning: The migration away from SHA-256 for the main Bitcoin network would require a massive, highly coordinated hard fork. It would render all existing SHA-256 ASICs (billions of dollars in hardware) obsolete, requiring catastrophic existential danger to justify such a move. Google's recent 'Willow' quantum chip only has 105 qubits, vastly below the millions of logical qubits required to break SHA-256 via Grover's algorithm or even break ECDSA signatures via Shor's algorithm. Because of this, there are no active Bitcoin Improvement Proposals (BIPs) aiming to remove SHA-256 being pushed, let alone ready to be deployed on the mainnet by May 31, 2025. Given Bitcoin's extremely conservative, slow-moving consensus process, executing a hard fork within a two-month timeframe without a live network-destroying zero-day exploit is effectively impossible. The market price for this was around 0.02, which reflects the baseline "miracle/troll" probability on prediction markets; I am confident the true probability is similarly at the floor of 0.02.
